hero.subtitle: Desenrole.ai helps you start, keep, and grow conversations on Instagram and WhatsApp — naturally, in your own style, no memorized scripts.
hero.disclaimer: Takes 2 minutes. No credit card needed for the quiz.

demo.received: I love traveling, just got back from the coast
demo.suggestion: The coast in the middle of winter? Either you're brave or you know a secret beach I need to hear about
demo.sent: The coast in the middle of winter? Either you're brave or you know a secret beach I need to hear about
demo.reply: haha both! you won't believe the place I found

how.title: From frozen to flowing in three steps
how.subtitle: No memorizing, no generic canned lines. The system understands the real context of your conversation.

step1.title: Share the context
step1.description: Paste the conversation or describe the situation: a story reply, a new match, a chat gone cold.

step2.title: Get suggestions in your tone
step2.description: The AI generates reply options that sound like you — playful, direct, or more reserved.

step3.title: Chat with confidence
step3.description: You pick, tweak, and send. Over time the freeze disappears — the skill becomes yours.

tools.title: From screenshot to the right reply
tools.subtitle: You send the screenshot, Desenrole shows you exactly what to do.

conversa.title: Analyze conversation
conversa.description: Send a WhatsApp or DM screenshot and find out exactly what to reply.

story.title: Analyze her Story
story.description: Send an Instagram Story screenshot and find the best way to catch her attention.

proof.title: People who used canned lines now have real conversations

t1.quote: I used to freeze on the first message. Two weeks with the analyzer and I understood what I was doing wrong. Three dates this month.
t1.name: Lucas M.

t2.quote: What changed for me was the tone. The suggestions sound like me talking, just without the anxiety getting in the way.
t2.name: Rodrigo A.

t3.quote: I thought it would be another pickup-lines app. It's the opposite: it taught me to notice signals I was completely missing.
t3.name: Felipe T.

pricing.title: Pick your pace
pricing.subtitle: Cancel anytime, right from your dashboard. No hassle.
pricing.guarantee: 7-day guarantee: not for you? Full refund, no questions asked.

q1.question: Isn't this manipulation?
q1.answer: No — and that's a non-negotiable principle of the product. Desenrole.ai is about communication: clarity, timing, reading context, and reducing anxiety. Suggestions are always respectful and sound like you. No pressure tactics, no games.

q2.question: Won't the messages sound artificial?
q2.answer: You set the tone — playful, direct, shy — and the AI adapts suggestions to how you talk. You can always edit before sending. The goal is to unblock you, not replace you.

q3.question: Which apps does it work with?
q3.answer: Any of them: Instagram, WhatsApp, Tinder, Bumble. You bring the conversation context and use the suggestions wherever you want.

q4.question: How do I cancel?
q4.answer: Two clicks, in the subscription area. Access continues until the end of the paid period. No phone calls, no retention emails, no tricks.

q5.question: Are my conversations safe?
q5.answer: Yes. Your conversations are private, never shared, and you can delete your history anytime. Privacy is built into the architecture.

finalCta.title: The next conversation can be different
finalCta.subtitle: Discover your communication profile in 2 minutes and see exactly where you're getting stuck.

premium.name: Premium
premium.description: Full access to every tool and game

footer.tagline: More natural conversations, more confidence, less anxiety.

q1.title: When a new conversation shows up — a match, someone who followed you, a story reply — what usually happens?

options.a: I think about the perfect message for so long I sometimes never send it
options.b: I send something quick, even without being sure it's the right approach
options.c: I wait for a clearer signal before acting
options.d: I send a good opener, but freeze when I need to keep the chat going

q2.title: After you send a message, what goes through your head while you wait for a reply?

options.a: I replay every word trying to guess if I said something wrong
options.b: Nothing much — I go on with my day
options.c: I analyze their reply pattern to gauge interest
options.d: I wonder if I should have sent something more

q3.title: A conversation went cold — the reply took a while or got too short. You...

options.a: Wait, and only react if she picks it back up
options.b: Send another message trying to revive it, even without knowing exactly how
options.c: Assume she lost interest and give up
options.d: Know you should send something light, but don't know what

q4.title: How do you usually decide it's time to ask someone out?

options.a: I go for it as soon as the conversation starts flowing
options.b: I wait for a very obvious signal before risking the invite
options.c: I overthink the perfect invite and end up postponing it
options.d: I know when it's time, but freeze when it comes to phrasing the invite

q5.title: What trips you up most in a conversation today?

options.a: Reading the right signals — I'm not always sure the interest is real
options.b: The anxiety between sending a message and getting a reply
options.c: I come on too strong and end up scaring people off
options.d: Keeping the momentum — I start well and lose the thread halfway

q6.title: If you could fix one thing right now, what would it be?

options.a: Being able to start without spending twenty minutes overthinking
options.b: Understanding better what the other person is signaling
options.c: Having more tact in the first messages
options.d: Not freezing when it's time to move the conversation forward

observador.title: The Cautious Reader
observador.tagline: You read the signals well, but wait too long for confirmation before acting.
observador.description: You have a real feel for conversational context — you pick up on tone, timing, and nuances most people miss. The issue isn't reading skill, it's over-caution: while you wait for a perfectly clear signal, the window sometimes closes.
strengths: Picks up on nuances most people miss
Rarely comes across as forced or rushed
Nails the right tone for each conversation
watchOuts: Waits for signals so obvious the moment passes
Can come across as uninterested from being too cautious
Hesitates right when it's time to move things forward

direto.title: The Unfiltered Direct One
direto.tagline: You've got initiative to spare — you just need to calibrate the tone.
direto.description: Freezing up isn't your problem — you act, start conversations, and propose next steps without hesitation. What's usually missing is calibration: reading the right moment before skipping steps, and adjusting your tone so it doesn't come off rushed or generic.
strengths: Never freezes out of fear of taking the first step
Can start a conversation from scratch without hesitating
Has the confidence to propose the next step
watchOuts: Sometimes skips steps before it's time
Can come across as rushed or generic
Doesn't always read interest before pushing forward

ansioso.title: The Anxious Perfectionist
ansioso.tagline: You know what you want to say — the anxiety is what gets in the way of sending it.
ansioso.description: You put real thought into every message — that's a strength. But chasing the perfect message, combined with the anxiety of waiting for a reply, throws off your timing: the delay sometimes cools down a conversation that was going well.
strengths: Puts real thought into every message
Genuinely cares about getting the tone right
Has the potential to be very present in a conversation
watchOuts: Freezes up so much it sometimes never gets sent
The delay can cool the chat without you noticing
Reads silence as rejection too quickly

quase-la.title: The Almost There
quase-la.tagline: You start strong — the challenge is keeping it going and closing it out.
quase-la.description: Your opener works: you start conversations naturally and have good timing. The blind spot shows up in the middle or the end — keeping the chat flowing, or taking the step to propose an actual date, which tends to freeze right at the key moment.
strengths: Has good timing for making first contact
Already has the basics down — just needs to unblock the rest
watchOuts: Loses the thread of the conversation halfway through
Freezes right when it's time to propose meeting up
Lets good conversations die out from indecision

faq.account: How do I change my email or password?
faq.accountAnswer: Go to Settings > Account. You can update your info and change your password anytime.
faq.cancel: How do I cancel my subscription?
faq.cancelAnswer: In Settings > Subscription. Access continues until the end of the paid period, no hassle.
faq.tools: How do I use the conversation tools?
faq.toolsAnswer: Pick a tool, upload a screenshot of the conversation or Story, and get ready-to-send suggestions. You can copy and tweak before sending.
faq.privacy: Is my data and conversations safe?
faq.privacyAnswer: Yes. Your conversations are private and never shared. You can delete your history anytime in Settings.
faq.billingAnswer: The subscription is recurring, weekly or monthly, and can be canceled anytime right from the dashboard.

Give my hand a long kiss like a ballroom farewell.
Challenge: whoever holds the stare longer earns a wish.
Say 'come here' in the most convincing way there is.
Fix my hair like it's an important ritual.
Name three things we'd do on a trip together.
Touch your nose to mine for five seconds.
Confess which part of my face you look at first.
Give a cinematic kiss — dipping allowed.
Make a request starting with 'tonight I want...'
Send the emoji that sums up this moment right now.
Say 'I won' and kiss me before I can argue.
Pick my weak spot and use it against me.
Finish the sentence: 'if you knew what I'm thinking...'
Kiss me like it's the last one of the night.
